comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Advisory E6 (unmeasurable 'spot-check' AC) addressed deterministically: curl -sIL each badge target returned HTTP 200 — pypi/v/nava-rebar, pypi/pyversions/nava-rebar, the Apache-2.0 badge, and the test.yml workflow badge.svg; PyPI project page 200; LICENSE file present as the license link target.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

Audit OSS finding #6 (MEDIUM). README.md has no badges — no PyPI version, supported-Python, license, or CI status indicator. For a package published to PyPI (nava-rebar) these are the fastest trust signals an evaluator scans for, and their absence makes an otherwise rigorous project look less maintained than it is. The repo already has everything the badges would advertise (PyPI releases, a 3.11/3.12 matrix, Apache-2.0, a green test workflow).

## What / files

Add a badge row at the top of README.md: PyPI version (shields.io pypi/v/nava-rebar), Python versions (pypi/pyversions/nava-rebar), license (Apache-2.0), and the test.yml workflow status badge from the GitHub mirror. Keep it to one line; no coverage or misc vanity badges unless already measured in CI. Note the GitHub Actions badge reflects the mirror's branch CI, which is accurate (Gerrit replicates main there and branch CI runs on push).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] README.md renders a single badge row: PyPI version, Python versions, license, CI status
- [ ] All badge URLs resolve (spot-check rendered README on the mirror)
- [ ] Badge targets link to PyPI project page, workflow runs, and LICENSE respectively
- [ ] No README content reflow beyond the added row
compiled_state.title: Add README badges (PyPI version, Python versions, license, CI status)
